DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-21-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Edward Miranda and Jason Dudas' demurrer to the second amended complaint (SAC) is overruled.

Movants contend that SAC counts 3 (negligent misrepresentation),4 (intentional misrepresentation) and 5 (fraud) are pled with insufficient particularity. I disagree; the SAC adequately pleads "how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Miranda's alleged representations as to insurance are adequately pled and the representations are not limited solely to insurance in any event. Agency theories are pled against Dudas.

Order is signed.

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-21-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Edward Miranda and Jason Dudas' motion to strike the second amended complaint (SAC) is denied.

First, movants seek to strike the entire SAC because it was filed two days late. No prejudice is claimed and our courts' policy is to decide cases on their merits.

Second, movants seek to strike SAC counts 4 (intentional misrepresentation) and 5 (fraud) because they were not in the first amended complaint. Plaintiff permissibly adds new theories of recovery on the same facts. (Pruitt v. Fontana (1956) 143 Cal.App.2d 675.) In any event, given that no trial date is set and California's pleadings rules are liberal, I would be bound to grant leave to amend if requested. (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89.)

Order is signed.
